meta.notes: Rust rewrite. Git repo required to run.
meta.tldr: This release introduces several key enhancements, including a built-in Amazon Bedrock model provider with configurable AWS profile support, streamlining model integration. The new `/mcp verbose` command offers comprehensive MCP server diagnostics without impacting the performance of the standard `/mcp` command. Plugin MCP loading now supports both `mcpServers` and top-level server maps in `.mcp.json`, providing greater flexibility in plugin configuration. Realtime handoffs are improved, allowing background agents to receive transcript deltas and selectively remain silent. Host-specific `remote_sandbox_config` requirements are now supported for remote environments, enhancing security. The Code Review skill has been refined with tightened instructions. A bug was fixed where `/copy` would copy a pre-rollback response. Input is now queued while shell commands run, preventing stuck states. Unicode input is fixed in VS Code WSL. Stale proxy environment variables are now prevented from being restored. `codex exec` now inherits root-level shared flags. Leaked review prompts are removed from TUI transcripts. This release also includes dependency updates and documentation improvements. It is recommended to upgrade to take advantage of these new features and bug fixes. No specific migration steps are required.

features.name: Interactive TUI
features.description: Full-screen terminal UI with conversation, approvals, transcript, and tool output panels.
key_details: @ in composer triggers fuzzy file search
Enter while Claude running: inject instructions mid-turn
Tab while running: queue follow-up for next turn
Esc Esc: edit previous user message / walk back transcript
$ mention picker: labels Skills, Apps, Plugins — plugins surface first

features.name: Non-Interactive Exec Mode
features.description: One-shot headless execution. codex exec runs without interactive TUI, outputs to stdout.
key_details: codex exec --yolo "task" — no sandbox, no approvals, live web search
codex exec resume --last "follow-up" — resume last session headlessly
codex exec resume <id> "follow-up" — resume specific session headlessly

features.name: Cloud Tasks
features.description: Run tasks in cloud environments. Best-of-N runs supported.
key_details: codex cloud exec --env ENV_ID --attempts 3 "task" — best-of-N runs

features.name: Session Resume
features.description: Resume prior sessions with transcript, plan history, and approvals preserved.
key_details: codex resume — picker of recent sessions
codex resume --all — sessions from any directory
codex resume --last — jump to most recent
Override CWD with --cd or add extra dirs with --add-dir

features.name: Multi-Agent
features.description: Codex auto-spawns sub-agents for parallel work. Manages orchestration, routing, and consolidation.

built_in_roles.purpose: Long-running command monitoring, up to 1hr wait calls

key_details: Consolidated response after all agents complete
/agent to switch between active agent threads
Interactive: approval requests surface from inactive threads (press o to open)
Non-interactive: unapproved actions fail and surface to parent

features.name: CSV Fan-Out (spawn_agents_on_csv)
features.description: Batch parallel processing. One worker per CSV row, results collected into output CSV.
key_details: Each worker calls report_agent_job_result exactly once
Output CSV includes original data + job metadata
features.example: Create /tmp/components.csv with columns path,owner. Call spawn_agents_on_csv with csv_path, instruction: 'Review {path} owned by {owner}. Return JSON.', output_csv_path: /tmp/review.csv

features.name: Custom Agent Roles
features.description: Define custom agent roles in config. Each role gets its own model, reasoning effort, sandbox policy, and developer instructions.
features.config_example:
[agents.reviewer]
description = "PR reviewer focused on correctness and security."
config_file = "agents/reviewer.toml"

features.name: Code Review
features.description: Built-in code review via /review command with preset review modes.
review_presets: Review against base branch — diff vs upstream
Review uncommitted changes — staged + unstaged + untracked
key_details: Override model with review_model in config

features.name: Web Search
features.description: Web search with cached results by default. --search flag for live results.

modes.cached: Default. OpenAI-maintained cached index (reduced prompt injection exposure).

features.name: Hooks Engine
features.description: Lifecycle hooks that fire at session start and stop events.

features.name: Code Mode
features.description: Isolated coding workflows with different execution context.

features.name: Plugin System
features.description: Curated marketplace with auth checks, rich metadata. Plugins provide tools, skills, and MCP servers.
key_details: Config switch to disable bundled system skills entirely (0.114.0)
$ mention picker labels Skills/Apps/Plugins, plugins surface first

features.name: MCP (Model Context Protocol)
features.description: STDIO and streaming HTTP MCP servers. Codex itself can run as MCP server for other agents.
key_details: Codex as MCP server: allows other agents to use Codex capabilities

features.name: TypeScript + Python SDK
features.description: Programmatic Codex control via TypeScript or Python SDKs.
features.typescript_example: const codex = new Codex(); const thread = codex.startThread(); await thread.run('Fix CI'); const thread2 = codex.resumeThread('<thread-id>');
key_details: Same thread: multiple sequential run() calls
Python SDK: generated v2 schema types added in 0.114.0
WebSocket server: GET /readyz and GET /healthz health endpoints (0.114.0)

features.name: Image Inputs
features.description: Attach images to queries. PNG and JPEG supported.
key_details: Paste images directly into interactive composer
Image generation also available (saves to CWD)

features.name: Permission Profiles
features.description: Precise filesystem and network sandbox policy configuration language. Replaces legacy permission modes.
key_details: Legacy workspace-write behavior preserved for backward compat (0.114.0 fix)
Profiles forward-compatible: newer profiles degrade safely on older builds

features.name: Feature Flags
features.description: Toggle experimental features per-profile.

features.name: SQLite Session Logging
features.description: Session transcripts stored in SQLite with configurable retention and pruning.

features.name: WebSocket Health Endpoints
features.description: WebSocket app-server deployments now expose `GET /readyz` and `GET /healthz` on the same listener for easier health checks.

features.name: Disable Bundled System Skills
features.description: Added a config switch to disable bundled system skills entirely.

features.name: Realtime Transcript Handoffs
features.description: Handoffs now carry realtime transcript context, which improves continuity when work is transferred between turns.

features.name: Improved Mention Picker
features.description: Improved the `$` mention picker by clearly labeling Skills, Apps, and Plugins, and by surfacing plugins first.

features.name: Full Resolution Image Inspection
features.description: Supported models can now request full-resolution image inspection through both `view_image` and `codex.emitImage(..., detail: "original")`, which helps with precision visual tasks.

features.name: js_repl cwd and homeDir helpers
features.description: `js_repl` now exposes `codex.cwd` and `codex.homeDir`, and saved `codex.tool(...)` / `codex.emitImage(...)` references keep working across cells.

features.name: Realtime Websocket Transcription and v2 Handoff
features.description: Realtime websocket sessions gained a dedicated transcription mode, plus v2 handoff support through the `codex` tool, with a unified `[realtime]` session config.

features.name: v2 App-Server Filesystem RPCs
features.description: The v2 app-server now exposes filesystem RPCs for file reads, writes, copies, directory operations, and path watching, and there is a new Python SDK for integrating with that API.

features.name: Smart Approvals Guardian Subagent
features.description: Smart Approvals can now route review requests through a guardian subagent in core, app-server, and TUI, reducing repeated setup work on follow-up approvals.

features.name: App Integrations Responses API
features.description: App integrations now use the Responses API tool-search flow, can suggest missing tools, and fall back cleanly when the active model does not support search-based lookup.

features.name: App-server TUI device-code ChatGPT sign-in
features.description: App-server TUI now supports device-code ChatGPT sign-in during onboarding and can refresh existing ChatGPT tokens.

features.name: Smoother Plugin Setup
features.description: Plugin setup is smoother: Codex can prompt to install missing plugins or connectors, honor a configured suggestion allowlist, and sync install/uninstall state remotely.

features.name: userpromptsubmit hook
features.description: Added a `userpromptsubmit` hook so prompts can be blocked or augmented before execution and before they enter history.

features.name: Realtime sessions thread context
features.description: Realtime sessions now start with recent thread context and are less likely to self-interrupt during audio playback.

features.name: Memory citation to agent message
features.description: Add memory citation to agent message

features.name: Plugins Workflow
features.description: Plugins are now a first-class workflow, allowing Codex to sync product-scoped plugins at startup, browse them in `/plugins`, and install or remove them with clearer auth/setup handling.

features.name: Sub-agents Path-Based Addresses
features.description: Sub-agents now use readable path-based addresses like `/root/agent_a`, with structured inter-agent messaging and agent listing for multi-agent v2 workflows.

features.name: Terminal Title Picker
features.description: The `/title` terminal-title picker now works in both the classic TUI and the app-server TUI, making parallel sessions easier to tell apart.

features.name: App-server Client Shell Commands
features.description: App-server clients can now send `!` shell commands, watch filesystem changes, and connect to remote websocket servers with bearer-token auth.

features.name: Smoother Image Workflows
features.description: Image workflows got smoother: `view_image` now returns image URLs for code mode, generated images are reopenable from the TUI, and image-generation history survives resume.

features.name: Prompt History Recall in App-server TUI
features.description: Prompt history recall now works in the app-server TUI, including across sessions.

features.name: Plugins Workflow V2
features.description: Codex can now sync product-scoped plugins at startup, browse them in `/plugins`, and install or remove them with clearer auth/setup handling.

features.name: Sub-agents Path-Based Addresses V2
features.description: Sub-agents now use readable path-based addresses like `/root/agent_a`, with structured inter-agent messaging and agent listing for multi-agent v2 workflows.

features.name: App-server Client Shell Commands V2
features.description: App-server clients can now send `!` shell commands, watch filesystem changes, and connect to remote websocket servers with bearer-token auth.

features.name: Smoother Image Workflows V2
features.description: Image workflows got smoother: `view_image` now returns image URLs for code mode, generated images are reopenable from the TUI, and image-generation history survives resume.

features.name: Prompt History Recall App-Server TUI V2
features.description: Prompt history recall now works in the app-server TUI, including across sessions.

features.name: Windows Sandbox Proxy-Only Networking
features.description: Windows sandbox runs can now enforce proxy-only networking with OS-level egress rules, instead of relying on environment variables alone.

features.name: ChatGPT Device Code Login for App Server
features.description: App-server clients can now start ChatGPT sign-in with a device code flow, which helps when browser callback login is unreliable or unavailable.

features.name: Prompt Plus Stdin Workflow for codex exec
features.description: `codex exec` now supports the prompt-plus-stdin workflow, so you can pipe input and still pass a separate prompt on the command line.

features.name: Dynamic Bearer Tokens for Custom Model Providers
features.description: Custom model providers can now fetch and refresh short-lived bearer tokens dynamically, instead of being limited to static credentials from config or environment variables.

features.name: Windows Sandbox Proxy-Only Networking with OS-Level Rules
features.description: Windows sandbox runs can now enforce proxy-only networking with OS-level egress rules, instead of relying on environment variables alone.

features.name: Realtime Voice Sessions v2 WebRTC
features.description: Realtime voice sessions now default to the v2 WebRTC path.
key_details: App-server coverage for the new flow

features.name: MCP Apps Richer Support
features.description: MCP Apps and custom MCP servers gained richer support.

features.name: Remote/App-Server Workflows Enhancements
features.description: Remote/app-server workflows now support egress websocket transport, remote `--cd` forwarding, runtime remote-control enablement, sandbox-aware filesystem APIs, and an experimental `codex exec-server` subcommand.

features.name: TUI Copy Latest Agent Response
features.description: The TUI can copy the latest agent response with `Ctrl+O`, including better clipboard behavior over SSH and across platforms.

features.name: Resume Jump to Session by ID/Name
features.description: `/resume` can now jump directly to a session by ID or name from the TUI.

features.name: TUI Configurable Notifications
features.description: TUI notifications are more configurable, including Warp OSC 9 support and an opt-in mode for notifications even while the terminal is focused.

features.name: Advanced Announcements per OS and Plans
features.description: Announcements can now be targeted per OS and plans.

features.name: Realtime Voice Sessions v2 WebRTC Default
features.description: Realtime voice sessions now default to the v2 WebRTC path.
key_details: App-server coverage for the new flow

features.name: TUI Copy Latest Agent Response (Ctrl+O)
features.description: The TUI can copy the latest agent response with `Ctrl+O`, including better clipboard behavior over SSH and across platforms.

features.name: Realtime Voice Sessions v2 WebRTC Configurable
features.description: Realtime voice sessions now default to the v2 WebRTC path, with configurable transport, voice selection, native TUI media support, and app-server coverage for the new flow.
key_details: App-server coverage for the new flow.

features.name: MCP Apps Richer Support
features.description: MCP Apps and custom MCP servers gained richer support, including resource reads, tool-call metadata, custom-server tool search, server-driven elicitations, file-parameter uploads, and more reliable plugin cache refreshes.

features.name: Remote/App-Server Workflows Enhancements
features.description: Remote/app-server workflows now support egress websocket transport, remote `--cd` forwarding, runtime remote-control enablement, sandbox-aware filesystem APIs, and an experimental `codex exec-server` subcommand.

features.name: TUI Copy Latest Agent Response with Ctrl+O
features.description: The TUI can copy the latest agent response with `Ctrl+O`, including better clipboard behavior over SSH and across platforms.

features.name: Resume Jump to Session by ID/Name
features.description: `/resume` can now jump directly to a session by ID or name from the TUI.
key_details: `/resume` command can jump to a session by ID or name.

features.name: TUI Configurable Notifications
features.description: TUI notifications are more configurable, including Warp OSC 9 support and an opt-in mode for notifications even while the terminal is focused.
key_details: Opt-in mode for notifications while the terminal is focused.

features.name: Realtime V2 Background Agent Progress Streaming
features.description: Realtime V2 can now stream background agent progress while work is still running and queue follow-up responses until the active response completes.

features.name: Improved Hook Activity in TUI
features.description: Hook activity in the TUI is easier to scan, with live running hooks shown separately and completed hook output kept only when useful.

features.name: Custom TUI Status Line with Thread Title
features.description: Custom TUI status lines can include the renamed thread title.

features.name: Code-mode Tool Declarations with Output Schema
features.description: Code-mode tool declarations now include MCP `outputSchema` details so structured tool results are typed more precisely.

features.name: SessionStart Hooks Clear Distinction
features.description: SessionStart hooks can distinguish sessions created by `/clear` from fresh startup or resume sessions.

features.name: Realtime V2 Background Agent Progress Streaming
features.description: Realtime V2 can now stream background agent progress while work is still running and queue follow-up responses until the active response completes.

key_details: Supports GitHub, git URLs, local directories, and direct `marketplace.json` URLs.

features.name: TUI Prompt History Improvements
features.description: Improves TUI prompt history, including `Ctrl+R` reverse search and local recall for accepted slash commands.
key_details: Enables local recall for accepted slash commands.

features.name: TUI and App-Server Memory Controls
features.description: Adds TUI and app-server controls for memory mode, memory reset/deletion, and memory-extension cleanup.

features.description: Expands MCP/plugin support with MCP Apps tool calls, namespaced MCP registration, parallel-call opt-in, and sandbox-state metadata for MCP servers.
key_details: Adds sandbox-state metadata for MCP servers.

Adds APIs for raw turn item injection.
Adds APIs for symlink-aware filesystem metadata.

features.name: Improved Standalone Installs
features.description: Standalone installs are now more self-contained, and `codex app` correctly opens or installs Desktop on Windows and Intel Macs.

features.name: TUI Side Conversations and Queued Input Enhancements
features.description: The TUI can now open `/side` conversations for quick side questions. Queued input now supports slash commands and `!` shell prompts while work is running.

features.name: Plan Mode with Fresh Context
features.description: Plan Mode can now start implementation in a fresh context, with context-usage shown before deciding whether to carry the planning thread forward.

features.name: Enhanced Plugin Workflows
features.description: Plugin workflows now include tabbed browsing, inline enable/disable toggles, marketplace removal, and remote, cross-repo, or local marketplace sources.

features.name: Filesystem Permissions Enhancements
features.description: Filesystem permissions now support deny-read glob policies, managed deny-read requirements, platform sandbox enforcement, and isolated `codex exec` runs that ignore user config or rules using `--ignore-user-config` and `--ignore-rules`.

features.name: Default Tool Discovery and Image Generation
features.description: Tool discovery and image generation are now enabled by default, with higher-detail image handling and original-detail metadata support for MCP and `js_repl` image outputs.

features.name: Standalone Installs Self-Contained
features.description: Standalone installs are more self-contained, and `codex app` now opens or installs Desktop correctly on Windows and Intel Macs.

features.name: Amazon Bedrock Model Provider
features.description: Added a built-in `amazon-bedrock` model provider with configurable AWS profile support.

features.name: Verbose MCP Diagnostics
features.description: Added `/mcp verbose` for full MCP server diagnostics, resources, and resource templates while keeping plain `/mcp` fast.

features.name: Flexible Plugin MCP Loading
features.description: Made plugin MCP loading accept both `mcpServers` and top-level server maps in `.mcp.json`.

features.name: Improved Realtime Handoffs
features.description: Improved realtime handoffs so background agents receive transcript deltas and can explicitly stay silent when appropriate.

features.name: Host-Specific Remote Sandbox Config
features.description: Added host-specific `remote_sandbox_config` requirements for remote environments.

commands.name: /review
commands.description: Open code review preset picker. Review branch diff, uncommitted changes, specific commit, or custom instructions.

commands.name: /experimental
commands.description: Toggle experimental features including multi-agent mode.

commands.name: /agent
commands.description: Switch between active agent threads in multi-agent mode.

commands.name: /clear
commands.description: Wipe terminal and start fresh chat.

commands.name: /fork
commands.description: Fork conversation from an earlier message.

commands.name: /title
commands.description: Allows the user to configure the terminal title.

commands.name: /marketplace
commands.description: Installs a plugin marketplace from a given source.

commands.name: /marketplace add
commands.description: Installs a plugin marketplace from a specified source.

commands.name: /side
commands.description: Opens a side conversation in the TUI.

commands.name: /marketplace remove
commands.description: Removes a plugin from the marketplace.

current.description: Auto-approve all actions within workspace. No permission prompts.
No sandbox, no approvals, live web search enabled.
Attach images to the initial query.
Force live web search (overrides cached default).
Specify cloud environment ID for cloud tasks.
Ignores user configuration during `codex exec` runs.

models.note: Fast status indicator shown in TUI for gpt-5.4 only

breaking_changes.title: Permission profiles behavior change
breaking_changes.description: Permission profiles forward-compatibility: newer profiles now degrade safely on older builds. Legacy workspace-write behavior preserved. Some configs that relied on older behavior may behave differently.
breaking_changes.migration: Review permission profile configs after upgrading. Test that sandbox policy behaves as expected. Use permission-profiles forward-compat config syntax.

breaking_changes.title: Legacy Artifact Tool Removal
breaking_changes.description: The legacy artifact tool and the old `read_file` and `grep_files` handlers have been removed.
breaking_changes.migration: Use alternative methods for accessing and processing files.

requirements: Git repository required (will not run outside trusted git directory)
PTY required for interactive TUI mode
